items.title: As some of you may be aware, Bluesky is switching from Tenor to Klipy for their gif hub.
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
As some of you may be aware, Bluesky is switching from Tenor to Klipy for their gif hub.

I’ve been having an issue where gifs I uploaded to Klipy are never viewable in Bluesky. The Klipy team says they are aware of the issue and should be fixed in the coming weeks.

images.alt:
Alt text:

Screenshot of an email from KLIPY Creators (creators@klipy.com) addressed to Hoop Informatics, received at 10:42 AM. The message reads: “Hello, Thanks for reaching out. Regarding your concern, we’re working on this and it should be fixed in the coming weeks. Thank you for using KLIPY. Thanks, KLIPY Team.” The sender name “KLIPY” in the sign-off appears as a hyperlink. The email is displayed in dark mode.

items.title: May 29, 1986: Bill Walton to Larry Bird to Jerry Sichting for two points during Boston's 117-95 win over Houston in Game 2 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 29, 1986: Bill Walton to Larry Bird to Jerry Sichting for two points during Boston's 117-95 win over Houston in Game 2 of the NBA Finals.

Bird finished with 31 PTS/8 REB/7 AST/4 STL/2 BLK and Kevin McHale had 25 PTS for the Celtics, who took a 2-0 series lead on the Rockets.

items.title: A wild upset in Paris.
items.author: Matt Norlander
items.body_excerpt:
A wild upset in Paris.

Janik Sinner and Novak Djokovic just got dropped on back-to-back days. João Fonseca, all of 19 years old, just came back from 2-0 to knock out Novak.

Guaranteed first-time major winner coming next win at the French Open.

images.alt: Novak Djokovic just pulled off the third-round upset at Roland Garros.

items.title: We'll see how he does in his first head-coaching opportunity, but I think the Magic made an outstanding move in hiring Sean Sweeney.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
We'll see how he does in his first head-coaching opportunity, but I think the Magic made an outstanding move in hiring Sean Sweeney.

He is one of the top associate or assistant coaches in the NBA. He's one of the best on the defensive side of the ball.

items.title: May 29, 2006: Shaquille O'Neal blocks Richard Hamilton's shot and takes it in for a layup during Miami's 89-78 win over Detroit in Game 4 of the Eastern Conference Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 29, 2006: Shaquille O'Neal blocks Richard Hamilton's shot and takes it in for a layup during Miami's 89-78 win over Detroit in Game 4 of the Eastern Conference Finals.

O'Neal finished with 21 PTS/9 REB and Dwyane Wade scored 31 PTS for the Heat, who took a 3-1 series lead.
